title: Creating a Home Study Space That Works
lead: You don't need a dedicated room. A consistent, distraction-free corner can make a real difference in focus and productivity.

sections.title: Consistency Matters More Than Size
content: The same spot each day signals 'study time' to the brain. A corner of the dining table, a small desk in the bedroom, or a spot at the kitchen counter—anywhere works if it's consistent.

sections.title: Light and Comfort
content: Good lighting reduces eye strain. A comfortable chair (but not so comfortable they fall asleep) helps. Keep supplies within reach so they're not constantly getting up.

sections.title: Minimize Distractions
content: Phones in another room or in a drawer. Turn off notifications. If siblings are noisy, consider headphones (music-free or low-fi) or scheduling study during quieter times.

sections.title: Involve Your Child
content: Let them help organize the space. A plant, a picture, or a favorite mug can make it feel like their spot. Ownership increases the chance they'll use it.

cta.title: Support Learning at Home
cta.text: GuruForU's online tutoring works from any quiet corner. Book a free session to try it.

faq.question: Do I need a dedicated room for a home study space?
faq.answer: No. A consistent corner—dining table, small desk, or kitchen counter—works. Consistency matters more than size; the same spot signals 'study time' to the brain.

faq.question: How can I minimize distractions during study?
faq.answer: Phones in another room or drawer, notifications off. If siblings are noisy, use headphones (music-free or low-fi) or schedule study during quieter times.

faq.question: What makes a study space effective?
faq.answer: Good lighting, a comfortable but not too comfortable chair, supplies within reach, and involvement from your child in organizing it. Ownership increases the chance they'll use it.

faq.question: Should my child help set up their study space?
faq.answer: Yes. Let them help organize. A plant, picture, or favorite mug can make it feel like their spot. Ownership increases engagement.
